sound_type: grunting moaning colony -- deep, grunting, moaning vocalizations at night. Colony chorus eerie and immersive. Nocturnal. E=4
ecosystem: Offshore islands, open ocean. Breeds on islands in burrows. Feeds in offshore waters. Nocturnal colony visitor.
season: Summer breeder on PNW islands. Present offshore year-round. Colony activity spring through fall.

coordinates.description: Rhinoceros Auklet colony on Protection Island, Strait of Juan de Fuca. ~36,000 nesting pairs. Nocturnal.

taxonomy_note.description: SECOND ALCIDAE. Continuing auk era from 174. Nocturnal colony behavior. Burrow nester. Horn-like casque at bill base. New genus: Cerorhinca (rhinoceros auklets).
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Cerorhinca from Greek 'keras' (horn) + 'rhynchos' (bill). monocerata from Greek 'monos' (single) + 'keras' (horn). Rhinoceros Auklet: the horn on the bill. Auklet: small auk.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Cerorhinca -- monotypic genus. Only one species. The horn-billed auklet. Closely related to puffins (Fratercula).
taxonomy_note.order_note: Charadriiformes. Alcidae continuing from degree 174. Second consecutive entry.
taxonomy_note.family_position: Second Alcidae. First nocturnal alcid in engine. Burrow nester. Horn morphology. Closely related to puffins.

song_metrics.song_duration_s: Individual calls 0.5-2 seconds. Colony chorus continuous for hours after dark.
song_metrics.note_structure: Deep grunts and moans: 'mmmrraah' and 'urrr-urrr-urrr.' No clear melodic structure. Guttural.
song_metrics.note_types: grunting/moaning calls (primary -- colony, nocturnal), bill clacking (defense), soft grunting (mate communication in burrow).
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Individual grunts and moans. Colony produces overlapping chorus. No structured phrases.
song_metrics.timbre: Deep, guttural, resonant. More felt than heard at distance. Eerie in collective nocturnal context.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=4. Individual moderate. Colony immersive. Nocturnal amplification of perceived intensity.
song_metrics.tempo: Irregular. Individual calls intermittent. Colony creates continuous low chorus.
song_metrics.context: Nocturnal colony return. Mate communication. Burrow defense. Breeding islands only.
song_metrics.key_property: GRUNTING MOANING COLONY -- nocturnal, deep, collective chorus. Horn casque on bill. Burrow nester. Protection Island 36K pairs. E=4. v1.49.315.

plumage.overall: Breeding: dark gray-brown overall. Orange bill with horn casque. White plumes behind eye and at bill base. Non-breeding: duller, horn reduced, plumes absent.
plumage.diagnostic_mark: Orange bill with horn casque (breeding). White facial plumes. Dark overall. Stocky build.
plumage.seasonal_variation: Breeding: horn present, white plumes, bright bill. Non-breeding: horn reduced, plumes absent, duller bill.
plumage.flight_pattern: Fast, direct, low over water. Short stiff wings. Rapid wingbeats. Typical alcid flight.

physical_description.overall: Length: 35-37 cm. Weight: 480-620g. Bill: orange with keratinized horn casque (breeding). Stocky build. Short wings.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Horn casque on orange bill. White facial plumes (breeding). Dark plumage. Stocky.
physical_description.field_id: Stocky dark seabird offshore. Orange bill with visible horn (breeding). Unlikely to see at colony (nocturnal). At sea: look for stocky dark alcid with orange bill.

nodes.title: Nocturnal Colony -- Night Chorus
nodes.description: Returns to colony only at night. Deep grunting/moaning chorus. Predator avoidance. Fish carrying in darkness. Navigation to correct burrow among thousands. Eerie immersive sound.

nodes.title: Rhinoceros Horn -- Bill Casque
nodes.description: Keratinized horn at bill base. Breeding adults. Orange bill. Named for this structure. Likely breeding condition signal. Cerorhinca = 'horn-bill.'

nodes.title: Burrow Nesting -- Underground Seabird
nodes.description: Digs burrows 1-5 meters into island soil. Strict island breeder. Predator-free islands required. Protection Island: 36,000 pairs. Catastrophic vulnerability to introduced predators.

nodes.title: Second Alcidae (174-175)
nodes.description: Guillemot (174): diurnal, nearshore, crevice, urban. Auklet (175): nocturnal, offshore, burrow, island. Two contrasting modes of alcid life documented.

cross_references.relationship: ALCIDAE SEQUENCE! First (174) vs second (175). Diurnal/nocturnal. Nearshore/offshore. Crevice/burrow. Urban/island. Two modes of alcid life.
ALCIDAE TRIO! Both nocturnal colony visitors. Both burrow nesters. Both on offshore islands. Closely related (Cerorhinca sister to Fratercula). Puffin: conservation crisis.
COLLECTIVE SOUND! Both produce collective sounds. Dunlin: wing-rush of thousands (diurnal). Auklet: moaning chorus of thousands (nocturnal). Different collective modes.
ACOUSTIC CONTRAST! Yellowlegs: solo sentinel, loud, diurnal alarm. Auklet: colony chorus, moderate, nocturnal moaning. Opposite acoustic strategies.
